summary.intent: Split a monolithic 1,655-line compaction_run.rs file into per-strategy submodules (Option A) to improve reviewer orientation and code organization on PR #653.
summary.outcome: Successfully refactored compaction_run.rs into a module directory with 7 files (mod.rs, shared.rs, deterministic.rs, recency_preserving.rs, structured.rs, llm_replace.rs, test_support.rs), with all 219 chat tests passing and fmt/clippy clean in both default and no-default-features configurations.

repo: The compaction pipeline supports four strategies: deterministic, recency_preserving, structured, and llm_replace — dispatched from run_compaction() in mod.rs.
The public API surface is run_compaction(), extract_summary_body(), and CompactionRunError — callers in lib.rs did not need changes after the split.
Tests are colocated with strategies in #[cfg(test)] mod tests blocks per file; shared test helpers live in test_support.rs.
The crate runs CI checks in two feature configurations: default and no-default-features — both must pass clippy and fmt.
This work is on PR #653 in the moltis repo, branch cstewart-hc/feat/deterministic-compaction.

code.finding: Contains HeadTailBounds, compute_boundaries, finalize_kept, in_place_prune_or_err, prune_tool_results_before, sanitize_tool_pairs, build_middle_marker, message_tokens, align_boundary_forward_past_tool_group — helpers consumed by multiple strategies.
Dispatcher and public API: run_compaction(), extract_summary_body(), CompactionRunError. Submodule declarations live here.

Contains STRUCTURED_TEMPLATE, STRUCTURED_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS, structured_first_compaction_instructions(), structured_iterative_instructions(), extract_previous_summary(), and structured_strategy() — largest strategy file at ~460 lines.
Shared test helpers used by both structured.rs and llm_replace.rs tests; required cfg(test) gating to fix clippy dead_code warnings.

workflow: Pure code-movement refactors (zero behavior change) are easiest to verify when committed separately — reviewers can confirm the diff is mechanical without scrutinizing logic.
Clippy must be run separately for each feature configuration (default and no-default-features) as errors can differ between them.
After a module-to-directory rename in Rust, the old .rs file must be explicitly deleted — cargo build won't error but the old file becomes dead code.
Running fmt before the final clippy pass avoids spurious formatting-related clippy findings.
Per-file line counts after a split are a useful sanity check — none exceeding 460 lines confirmed the split was balanced.

friction: Multiple rounds of clippy errors required iterative fixes across structured.rs, llm_replace.rs, recency_preserving.rs, and shared.rs after the initial split.
fmt failures required additional edit passes on structured.rs and recency_preserving.rs after clippy was already clean.
test_support.rs needed explicit cfg(test) annotation to silence dead_code clippy warnings since it's only used in test contexts.
open_items: PR #653 still under review — the refactor commit was added on top of the existing feature work, making it a large-diff PR.
Future strategy additions (e.g., auxiliary-model support mentioned as 'moltis-8me') will land as new files in the compaction_run/ directory.
